page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs based on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

For connectivity, the c8gn.8xlarge offers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-bound and EBS-reliant workloads. In synthetic benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier memory performance, with uncached read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 390.52 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 185.7 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21777.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 47220.04, both placing in the top tier of tested hardware. Single-core performance is also robust, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1893.0 and a bzip3 decompression rate of 14.58 GB/sec.

The instance's hardware profile presents distinct architectural tradeoffs. While it excels in memory bandwidth and multi-threaded integer operations, such as software compilation and database tasks, it lacks x86 instruction sets like AVX or AMX, and shows weaker performance in large-scale LLM inference, with 70B model prompt processing dropping to 5.9 tokens/sec. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom ARMv9 architecture delivers high resource density and top-tier memory throughput without the premium cost of traditional x86 architectures. This makes the c8gn.8xlarge highly suitable for network-intensive applications, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded software compilation.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 64.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core), and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. Lacking local storage and GPUs, the instance relies on a 100 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmarks reveal top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached reads at 390.52 GB/sec and writes at 185.7 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core capabilities, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 21777.0 and high database operation throughput. While it exhibits lower performance on large-scale LLM inference due to the lack of specialized accelerators, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated core allocation make it highly cost-efficient for network-intensive workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 100 Gbps network baseline for high-throughput database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized arm64 server with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance delivering 100 Gbps network bandwidth and top-tier memory throughput for high-concurrency database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 cores running at 2.8 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 390.52 GB/sec uncached reads
Best-fit for multi-threaded compilation, databases, and web serving
